Event: Tom blows millions of dollars on flashy, pointless expenses for Entertainment 720 without defining the business purpose
Explanation: Squanders large resources on nonfunctional showiness, leading to bankruptcy and no lasting benefit.

Event: Tom neglects essential work and takes shortcuts in his early business ventures
Explanation: His laziness causes his ventures to fail, hurting his own prospects and wasting others’ time and money.

Event: Tom slowly works to outgrow his laziness and become a successful businessman
Explanation: He deliberately self-improves and eventually achieves more stable success that benefits collaborators.

Event: Tom invests in a gay bar
Explanation: He likely gains profit and experience while providing a social venue and service.

Event: Tom drinks alcohol despite being a lightweight and gets drunk
Explanation: He harms his own functioning with little benefit; impact on others is minor but not clearly positive.

Event: Tom tells Ben they should get Joan Callamezzo safely home when she’s drunk
Explanation: He gains minor moral credit but primarily protects Joan from harm.

Event: Tom marries Wendy so she can obtain citizenship
Explanation: He gains companionship/status and she gains a green card; no harm is indicated.

Event: Tom wears pink and “peacocks” with fashion to stand out
Explanation: He boosts his own image and networking with only negligible effects on others.

Event: Tom reveals in “Jerry’s Retirement” that he was the “Jerry” of his previous office and reinvented himself
Explanation: His reinvention helps his self-esteem and gives others context for his behavior without clear harm.

Event: Tom picks on and bullies Garry to keep him at the bottom of the office hierarchy
Explanation: He gains status and deflects ridicule at significant emotional cost to Garry.

Event: Tom carefully tries to avoid becoming the “new Jerry” after Garry retires
Explanation: His anxious maneuvering backfires, lowering his status while not helping anyone else.

Event: Tom goes to great lengths to find someone else (Andy, then an intern) to be the new “Jerry”
Explanation: Attempts to offload humiliation just make him look more pathetic without securing an actual benefit.

Event: Tom accidentally shoots Ron in the back of the head on a hunting trip because he doesn’t check his field of fire
Explanation: Reckless carelessness nearly causes severe injury and exposes him to consequences with no gain.

Event: Tom, trying to distract from who shot Ron, opens fire on someone else and almost kills them
Explanation: He compounds the danger, risking more serious harm and legal trouble without solving his problem.

Event: Tom lets Leslie take the fall for shooting Ron
Explanation: He shields himself from punishment by shifting blame and risk onto Leslie.

Event: Tom obnoxiously broadcasts that Ann is out of his league to everyone during their relationship
Explanation: His insecurity display undermines his own relationship and annoys Ann until she leaves.

Event: Tom turns Leslie’s “Meet ’N’ Greet” into a pitch for Entertainment 720
Explanation: He hijacks her event to chase funding for himself at the expense of her political goal.

Event: Tom carelessly spends Entertainment 720’s money on excessive props and surplus attractive employees without doing real work
Explanation: He burns capital and endangers jobs while failing to build a sustainable business.

Event: Tom changes his birth name Darwish Sabir Ismael Gani to “Tom Haverford” for political viability
Explanation: The rebranding gives him better career prospects and makes it easier for others to accept him in politics.

Event: Tom frequently assigns nicknames to Ron
Explanation: Light teasing and branding reinforce rapport and humor with no serious downside.

Event: Tom secretly donates all the tips he earned from the Venezuelans to the park fund
Explanation: He sacrifices immediate cash for reputation and the park’s benefit.

Event: Tom rents his stylish clothes to middle school students through Rent-a-Swag
Explanation: He finds a niche market, earning income and giving kids affordable fashion access.

Event: Tom continues trying new ventures after failures, showing persistence
Explanation: His resilience improves his eventual success and creates future services for others.

Event: Tom plays music at “Prom” that is out of touch with high school students’ tastes
Explanation: His misjudgment lowers his own social standing and reduces others’ enjoyment.

Event: Tom cultivates a Jay-Z / T-Pain-inspired swagger and slang to seem cool
Explanation: His image work gives him limited benefit yet often comes off awkwardly to others.

Event: Tom repeatedly schemes to get rich through new plans and inventions that usually fail
Explanation: His repeated failed schemes waste his own effort and some others’ time, with few clear gains.

Event: Tom writes a massively successful self-help book based on his failures
Explanation: He transforms past losses into major personal success while providing useful guidance.

Event: Tom boasts that he rides others’ coattails and takes partial credit
Explanation: His self-admission undermines his own image and slightly sours relationships without real upside.

Event: Tom refuses to give up his “thinking PJs” and “YouTube blazer,” treating them as serious tools
Explanation: His personal rituals aid his confidence and focus with no meaningful effect on others.

Event: Tom creates and runs Rent-a-Swag successfully before being bought out
Explanation: He profits and fills a market gap, and the buyout suggests mutual economic benefit.

Event: Tom returns to city government and creates the Business Liaison position to bring outside businesses to Pawnee
Explanation: He advances his career while facilitating economic growth for the town.

Event: Tom uses his marketing talent in “Fluoride” to beat Councilman Jamm at his own game
Explanation: He gains political capital and helps pass a beneficial policy for the public.

Event: Tom gradually becomes kinder over time
Explanation: His emotional growth improves his relationships and the well-being of those around him.
